data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0013/sessions/0037-horizon-T9

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation for Horizon (run 0013, T9)

- **Active frontier — Milne Lemma 3.3.** Useful context: the sole open Albanese extension-leg sorry is `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:1662`), gating `Thm32RationalMapExtension` → `ALB.up`. Both difference-map primitives now exist: brick (a) `RationalMap.precomp` (`Albanese/RationalMapPrecomp.lean`) and brick (b) `RationalMap.prod` (`Albanese/RationalMapProd.lean`) — both sorry-free, Ground-kernel-verified axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, build green (8559 jobs). Glue `precomp_hom_toRationalMap` landed alongside.

- **Next assembly steps (ordered).** Substep-1 remainder in memory `t9-albanese-endgame-unblock-map`: (ii) build `ha`/`hb` for `prod` at `sX := pr₁ ≫ X.hom`; (iii) projection openness `IsOpenMap (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).base` (instance chain Smooth→Flat+LFP→UniversallyOpen→IsOpenMap); (iv) difference morphism `d = fst/snd : G⊗G ⟶ G` via `CategoryTheory.MonObj`/`Hom.group`; (v) product integrality, whose one genuine new lemma is `Smooth ⟹ GeometricallyReduced` (Mathlib v4.31 has only the converse). Then substep 2 (slice) and 4b (diagonal codim-1 Krull bound) are the substantive remainder.

- **Blueprint/build notes.** The cluster is pinned in `Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` `sec:milne_lem33` (`def:rationalMap_precomp`, `lem:rationalMap_precomp_compHom`, `lem:rationalMap_precomp_hom`, `def:rationalMap_prod`, `lem:rationalMap_prod_proj`), all `\uses`-wired into the Milne 3.3 proof — no dangling. New Albanese leaves import only Mathlib and kernel-build standalone; a root `lake build` risks concurrent Picard-module churn.

- **Separate cone (not on T9 path).** `I-0118` records that pinned `Scheme.QuotScheme` is false-as-written (properness ≠ Nitsure's projective hypothesis; v4.31 lacks very-ampleness vocabulary); blueprint `thm:quot_representable` is `\notready`. This is an `AJC.picrep`/T12 concern.

# Task focus
Task: T9
Title: Jacobian⇄Albanese endgame glue
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean,blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese*.tex,blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex

If representability is not yet proven, don't try to fill its sorries, this is another task, just work on the albanese part. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with $k$-rational point, the Jacobian $J = \mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}$ satisfies the Albanese universal property
$$\forall\, (A,a)\ \text{pointed abelian variety},\quad \mathrm{Hom}_{\ast}(C,A)\ \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\ \mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{AbVar}}(J,A),$$
and is the unique such object up to canonical isomorphism.

You may read the Albanese subproject to see whether there has already been progress not updated with this project.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). Also `Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (specialisation of the substrate), both `isAbelianVariety` assemblies (the `Pic0Scheme`-namespace one MOVED to `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`), and `tangentSpaceCotangentDual` now hypothesis-free.
- **Still blocked, do not re-probe**: `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` QC∧GeomIrred conjunct (EGA IV₂ 4.5.8/4.6.1 translate-cover argument, not in Mathlib v4.31); `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` (Quot/Hilbert χ endgame); `finrank_eq_genus`, `Pic0.smooth`, `Pic0.proper` (need tangentSpaceIso resp. Chevalley–Rosenlicht).
- **tangentSpaceIso attack path opened**: the ring-level truncated-exponential splitting is DONE (`Picard/DualNumberUnits.lean`, axiom-clean: `(R[ε])ˣ ≃* Rˣ × (R,+)` + naturality via `mapRingHom`; blueprint `sec:pic0_trunc_exp`, 15 nodes). 1629	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1630	
1631	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1632	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1633	`thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus
1634	to be empty when the target is an abelian variety, at which point
1635	`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` extends.
1636	
1637	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1638	
1639	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1640	codim 1.**
1641	
1642	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1643	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1644	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1645	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or of *pure codimension 1*,
1646	encoded as: every point of `Z(f)` lies in the closure of a codim-1 point
1647	*of `Z(f)` itself*. (The `z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f` conjunct is essential:
1648	without it the disjunct is nearly vacuous — on a variety every non-generic
1649	point specialises from *some* codim-1 point — and, in particular, too weak
1650	to combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 to force `Z(f) = ∅`
1651	in Theorem 3.2. Statement strengthened in run-0006 T6, session 0015.)
1652	
1653	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1654	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1655	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1656	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1657	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1658	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1659	
1660	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1661	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1662	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1663	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1664	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1665	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1666	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1667	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1668	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1669	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1670	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1671	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1672	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
1673	    (_hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
1674	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1675	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1676	        ∃ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1677	          Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1678	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the

1682	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1683	  --
1684	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1685	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1686	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1687	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1688	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1689	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1690	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1691	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1692	  --   the domain of definition.
1693	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1694	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1695	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that

1697	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1698	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is

All of `AlbaneseUP.lean`'s `abelJacobi`/`SymmetricPower`/`symmetricPowerAVMap`/`symmetricPowerToJacobian`/`descentThroughBirationalSigma`/`albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq` are downstream. Building `Sym^g C` (Milne III.3, glue `Spec((A^{⊗g})^{S_g})`) is a large multi-session sub-build. `bundle` (A.3 representability) is out of T9 scope. `albanese_universal_property` is ALREADY sorry-free assembly from the two theorem sorries.

(run 0013 session 0022.)** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.{stalkPullback, fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint, functionFieldPullback}` (`Albanese/RationalMapFunctionField.lean`, all axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, committed `0db13b411b`, blueprint-pinned `lem:fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` + `def:functionFieldPullback` `3805df7906`). `stalkPullback` = germ pullback `𝒪_{Y,f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)`; the **dominant refinement** is now DONE: for `[IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y] (f : X ⤏ Y) [f.IsDominant]`, `fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` proves `f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X)) = genericPoint Y`, so `functionFieldPullback : Y.functionField ⟶ X.functionField` via `Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq …) .hom ≫ f.stalkPullback`. The two flagged "walls" were NOT walls: `haveI : Nonempty ↥g.domain := ⟨⟨genericPoint X, hη⟩⟩` where `hη := (genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec`, then `IsIntegral g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance` (mathlib `[IsIntegral X][Nonempty U]→IsIntegral U`) gives `IrreducibleSpace` free. Recipe: `obtain ⟨g,rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep` + `(PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp ‹_›`; `rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]`; `have : PartialMap.fromFunctionField g = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη ≫ g.hom := rfl` then `rw [·, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]`; inner point via `(g.domain.ι).isOpenEmbedding.injective` + `genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion` + `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` + `Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`; dominant image via `(genericPoint_spec _).image g.hom.continuous` + `Set.image_univ` + `g.hom.denseRange.closure_range` + `IsGenericPoint.eq (genericPoint_spec Y)`. Skeptical note: `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` is UNPINNED, so the field hom closes nothing directly — it enables a future `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`.

So the difference map `Φ = m∘(f×f) : X×X ⇢ G` needs new project-side primitives: (a) precompose a rational map with the projections `pr_i : X×X → X` (an open dominant morphism — needs "preimage of dense open under open map is dense"), and (b) a pairing `X×X ⇢ G×G` into a product target. THEN substeps 2/3/4b. `functionFieldPullback` supplies only the substep-3 K(Y)→K(X) piece for a *dominant* map (Milne's Φ pulls back `𝒪_{G,e}` at `Φ(x,x)=e`, a germ-at-a-point criterion, not the generic-point field hom) — so even substep 3 needs a germ-definedness criterion, not this. Net: closing `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` is ≥ a multi-session infra build.

Also `PartialMap.precomp` (`@[simps domain hom]`, domain `p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain`, hom `(p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ f.hom`), `precomp_equiv`, `precomp_toRationalMap`, `precomp_compHom` (`(f∘p)∘g = (f∘g)∘p`), and instance `precomp_isOver` (f∘p over S when f,p over S — so Φ is over k̄). Recipes that landed: the `(f.precomp p hp).domain` vs `p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain` transparency wall is beaten by `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` (mathlib's own RationalMap idiom; must precede the docstring, not follow it); `homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict` (restriction square, proved by `← cancel_mono U.ι` + `morphismRestrict_ι`/`homOfLE_ι`); `↑U ↘ S` unfolds (defeq) to `U.ι ≫ (X↘S)` via the priority-900 `CanonicallyOverClass→OverClass` instance, so `change` + `comp_over` closes the IsOver restriction goal. Blueprint: `def:rationalMap_precomp` + `lem:rationalMap_precomp_compHom` in `Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`, wired into Milne 3.3 sub-step 1.
21	- **BRICK (b) pairing LANDED + verified (run 0013 session 0032-T9, commit `38d3bfb948`).** New leaf `Albanese/RationalMapProd.lean` (imports only Mathlib, kernel-built 8558 jobs, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`). `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.prod (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S) [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ] (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z) (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) : X ⤏ pullback sY sZ`. **DESIGN CHANGE from the scoped plan: dropped the `IsOver`/`equivFunctionFieldOver` typeclass route in favour of EXPLICIT structure morphisms `sX,sY,sZ` + `RationalMap.ofFunctionField` + `pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField`.** Reason: a global `OverClass (pullback (Y↘S)(Z↘S)) S` would diamond against the canonical `canonicallyOverPullback` (which is over `Z` via `snd`, `Pullbacks.lean:813`), and the over-S property is cleaner as the explicit eqn `a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap` (= mathlib `RationalMap.isOver_iff`). 1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Pairing of two rational maps into a fibre product
10	
11	Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` API provides `compHom`
12	(right-composition with a morphism) but no way to *pair* two rational maps out of
13	a common source into a fibre product. For Milne's difference-map construction
14	`Φ = m ∘ (f × f) : X × X ⤏ G` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Lemma 3.3) one needs
15	exactly this pairing: given `f ∘ pr₁` and `f ∘ pr₂ : X × X ⤏ G`, assemble the map
16	`X × X ⤏ G ×_{k̄} G` whose two projections recover the factors, so that it can be
17	right-composed with the group law `G ×_{k̄} G → G`.
18	
19	The naive `PartialMap`-level pairing via `pullback.lift` does **not** descend to
20	rational maps through `Quotient.map₂`: `RationalMap.IsOver S` guarantees only *one*
21	over-`S` representative, not that an arbitrary pair of representatives is
22	simultaneously over `S`, so the compatibility hypothesis fails. Instead we use the
23	function-field correspondence: over an integral source `X`, a rational map
24	`X ⤏ Y` over `S` is the same datum as an `S`-morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y`
25	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.ofFunctionField` /
26	`equivFunctionField`), and morphisms out of the single scheme `Spec K(X)` pair
27	freely by `pullback.lift`.
28	
29	To stay clear of `Over`-typeclass diamonds on the pullback target we work with
30	explicit structure morphisms `sX : X ⟶ S`, `sY : Y ⟶ S`, `sZ : Z ⟶ S` (matching
31	mathlib's `equivFunctionField`), and phrase the over-`S` datum as the equation
32	`a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`. In Milne's setting `S = Spec k̄`,
33	`sX = X.hom`, `sY = sZ = G.hom`, and the target `pullback G.hom G.hom` is
34	definitionally `(G ⊗ G).left` (`CategoryTheory.Over.tensorObj_left`), so the group
35	law `(GrpObj.mul).left : (G ⊗ G).left ⟶ G.left` composes on the right directly.
36	
37	## Main definitions
38	
39	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod` — the pairing `X ⤏ pullback sY sZ` of `a : X ⤏ Y` and
40	  `b : X ⤏ Z`, both over `S`.
41	
42	## Main results
43	
44	* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_compHom` — the function-field morphism of a
45	  right-composition is the composite of function-field morphisms.
46	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod_compHom_fst` / `prod_compHom_snd` — the two projections of
47	  the pairing recover the original rational maps.
48	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod_compHom_over` — the pairing is again over `S`.
49	-/
50	
51	set_option autoImplicit false
52	
53	universe u
54	
55	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
56	
57	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
58	
59	namespace Scheme
60	
61	namespace RationalMap
62	
63	variable {T X Y Z S : Scheme.{u}}
64	
65	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
66	/-- **Associativity of right-composition.** Composing on the right by `g` and then `h`
67	is composing by `g ≫ h`. -/
68	@[simp]
69	lemma compHom_compHom (f : X ⤏ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T) :
70	    (f.compHom g).compHom h = f.compHom (g ≫ h) := by
71	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
72	  simp only [← compHom_toRationalMap]
73	  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
74	  simp only [PartialMap.compHom_hom, Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp,
75	    Category.assoc]
76	
77	/-- **Function-field functoriality of right-composition.** For a rational map
78	`f : X ⤏ Y` out of an irreducible scheme and a morphism `g : Y ⟶ Z`, the associated
79	morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Z` of `f.compHom g` is `f.fromFunctionField ≫ g`. -/
80	@[simp]
81	lemma fromFunctionField_compHom [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X ⤏ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
82	    (f.compHom g).fromFunctionField = f.fromFunctionField ≫ g := by
83	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
84	  rw [← compHom_toRationalMap, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
85	  exact PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_compHom f g _ _
86	
87	/-- The function-field morphism of an over-`S` rational map factors the structure
88	morphism: if `a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`, then
89	`a.fromFunctionField ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk η ≫ sX`. -/
90	lemma fromFunctionField_comp_structure [IsIntegral X] (a : X ⤏ Y) (sX : X ⟶ S)
91	    (sY : Y ⟶ S) (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) :
92	    a.fromFunctionField ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk (genericPoint X) ≫ sX := by
93	  rw [← fromFunctionField_compHom, ha]
94	  exact PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_toPartialMap sX _
95	
96	/-- **Pairing of two rational maps into a fibre product.** For rational maps
97	`a : X ⤏ Y` and `b : X ⤏ Z` out of an integral scheme `X`, both defined over `S`
98	(encoded as `a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`, resp. for `b`), the pairing
99	`a.prod b : X ⤏ pullback sY sZ` is the rational map whose function-field morphism is
100	`pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField`.
101	
102	Milne's difference map is `(f.precomp pr₁).prod (f.precomp pr₂)` followed by
103	right-composition with the group law of `G`. -/
104	noncomputable def prod (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
105	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
106	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
107	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
108	    X ⤏ pullback sY sZ :=
109	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) :=
110	    MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ _ _ inferInstance inferInstance
111	  ofFunctionField sX (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY)
112	    (pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField
113	      ((fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha).trans
114	        (fromFunctionField_comp_structure b sX sZ hb).symm))
115	    (by rw [pullback.lift_fst_assoc]; exact fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha)
116	
117	/-- The function-field morphism of the pairing is the `pullback.lift` of the factors'
118	function-field morphisms. -/
119	@[simp]
120	lemma prod_fromFunctionField (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
121	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
122	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
123	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
124	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).fromFunctionField
125	      = pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField
126	        ((fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha).trans
127	          (fromFunctionField_comp_structure b sX sZ hb).symm) := by
128	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) :=
129	    MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ _ _ inferInstance inferInstance
130	  exact fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField _ _ _ _
131	
132	/-- **First projection of the pairing.** Right-composing the pairing with
133	`pullback.fst` recovers `a`. -/
134	lemma prod_compHom_fst (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
135	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
136	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
137	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
138	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.fst sY sZ) = a :=
139	  eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq _ _ (by
140	    rw [fromFunctionField_compHom, prod_fromFunctionField, pullback.lift_fst])
141	
142	/-- **Second projection of the pairing.** Right-composing the pairing with
143	`pullback.snd` recovers `b`. -/
144	lemma prod_compHom_snd (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
145	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
146	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
147	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
148	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.snd sY sZ) = b :=
149	  eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq _ _ (by
150	    rw [fromFunctionField_compHom, prod_fromFunctionField, pullback.lift_snd])
151	
152	/-- **The pairing is over `S`.** Right-composing with the structure morphism
153	`pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY` of the fibre product recovers `sX.toRationalMap`. -/
154	lemma prod_compHom_over (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
155	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
156	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
157	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
158	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) = sX.toRationalMap := by
159	  rw [← compHom_compHom, prod_compHom_fst, ha]
160	
161	end RationalMap
162	
163	end Scheme
164	
165	end AlgebraicGeometry
166

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism
10	
11	Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` API provides only `compHom`,
12	the composition of a rational map with a *morphism on the right*
13	(`f : X ⤏ Y`, `g : Y ⟶ Z` ↦ `X ⤏ Z`). The dual operation — precomposing a
14	rational map with a morphism *on the left* — is missing.
15	
16	For a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying continuous map is **open**, and a
17	rational map `f : X ⤏ Y`, the composite `f ∘ p : W ⤏ Y` is a well-defined
18	rational map. Openness of `p` is exactly what guarantees that the preimage
19	`p⁻¹(f.domain)` of the (dense open) domain of definition stays dense
20	(`Dense.preimage`), so that the pulled-back partial map is again a partial map,
21	and that the construction descends to equivalence classes.
22	
23	This is the first missing brick in Milne's difference-map construction
24	`Φ = m ∘ (f × f) : X × X ⤏ G` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Lemma 3.3): the two
25	projections `prᵢ : X × X ⟶ X` are open (flat, locally of finite presentation,
26	being base changes of the smooth structure morphism `X ⟶ Spec k̄`), so
27	`f.precomp prᵢ` is the pullback of `f` along a projection.
28	
29	## Main definitions
30	
31	* `Scheme.PartialMap.precomp` — precomposition of a partial map with an open
32	  morphism.
33	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp` — precomposition of a rational map with an open
34	  morphism, descended from `PartialMap.precomp`.
35	
36	## Main results
37	
38	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp_compHom` — precomposition commutes with
39	  right-composition, `(f.precomp p hp).compHom g = (f.compHom g).precomp p hp`.
40	-/
41	
42	set_option autoImplicit false
43	
44	universe u
45	
46	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
47	
48	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
49	
50	namespace Scheme
51	
52	variable {W X Y Z : Scheme.{u}}
53	
54	namespace PartialMap
55	
56	/-- **Auxiliary restriction square.** For a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` and opens
57	`V ≤ U` of `X`, restricting `p ∣_ U` to the smaller preimage `p ⁻¹ᵁ V` agrees
58	with `p ∣_ V` followed by the inclusion `V ↪ U`. Both sides are pinned down by
59	composing with the mono `U.ι`. -/
60	lemma homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict (p : W ⟶ X) {U V : X.Opens} (hUV : V ≤ U)
61	    (h : p ⁻¹ᵁ V ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
62	    W.homOfLE h ≫ (p ∣_ U) = (p ∣_ V) ≫ X.homOfLE hUV := by
63	  rw [← cancel_mono U.ι, Category.assoc, morphismRestrict_ι,
64	    ← Category.assoc (W.homOfLE h), Scheme.homOfLE_ι, Category.assoc,
65	    Scheme.homOfLE_ι, morphismRestrict_ι]
66	
67	/-- **Precomposition of a partial map with an open morphism.** For `f : X ⤏ Y`
68	(as a partial map) and a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying map is open, the
69	composite `f ∘ p : W ⤏ Y` is defined on `p⁻¹(f.domain)`, which is dense because
70	open maps pull dense sets back to dense sets. -/
71	@[simps domain hom]
72	noncomputable def precomp (f : X.PartialMap Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
73	    W.PartialMap Y where
74	  domain := p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain
75	  dense_domain := f.dense_domain.preimage hp
76	  hom := (p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ f.hom
77	
78	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
79	/-- Precomposition respects the equivalence of partial maps, hence descends to
80	rational maps. -/
81	lemma precomp_equiv {f g : X.PartialMap Y} (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base)
82	    (h : f.equiv g) : (f.precomp p hp).equiv (g.precomp p hp) := by
83	  obtain ⟨V, hV, hVl, hVr, e⟩ := h
84	  have hVd : Dense ((p ⁻¹ᵁ V : W.Opens) : Set W) := by
85	    rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe]; exact hV.preimage hp
86	  refine ⟨p ⁻¹ᵁ V, hVd, p.preimage_mono hVl, p.preimage_mono hVr, ?_⟩
87	  simp only [restrict_hom, precomp_hom] at e ⊢
88	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc,
89	    homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict p hVl, homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict p hVr,
90	    Category.assoc, Category.assoc, e]
91	
92	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
93	/-- Precomposition with an `S`-morphism preserves being an `S`-map: if `f` is an
94	`S`-rational map and `p : W ⟶ X` is an `S`-morphism, then `f ∘ p` is an
95	`S`-rational map. In Milne's setting all schemes live over `k̄`, so the difference
96	map is automatically a `k̄`-map. -/
97	instance precomp_isOver {S : Scheme.{u}} [W.Over S] [X.Over S] [Y.Over S]
98	    (f : X.PartialMap Y) [f.IsOver S] (p : W ⟶ X) [p.IsOver S] (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
99	    (f.precomp p hp).IsOver S := by
100	  haveI hrestr : (p ∣_ f.domain).IsOver S := by
101	    rw [Hom.isOver_iff]
102	    change (p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ (f.domain.ι ≫ X ↘ S) = (p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain).ι ≫ W ↘ S
103	    rw [← Category.assoc, morphismRestrict_ι, Category.assoc, comp_over]
104	  change ((p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ f.hom).IsOver S
105	  infer_instance
106	
107	end PartialMap
108	
109	/-- **Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism.** For `f : X ⤏ Y`
110	and a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying map is open, `f.precomp p hp : W ⤏ Y`
111	is the composite `f ∘ p`, obtained by pulling any representative partial map back
112	along `p`. -/
113	noncomputable def RationalMap.precomp (f : X ⤏ Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
114	    W ⤏ Y :=
115	  Quotient.map (PartialMap.precomp · p hp) (fun _ _ ↦ PartialMap.precomp_equiv p hp) f
116	
117	@[simp]
118	lemma RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap (f : X.PartialMap Y) (p : W ⟶ X)
119	    (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
120	    (f.precomp p hp).toRationalMap = f.toRationalMap.precomp p hp := rfl
121	
122	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
123	/-- Precomposition on the left commutes with right-composition by a morphism.
124	This is what lets Milne's difference map be assembled as
125	`(f.precomp prᵢ).compHom (m ∘ (id × inv))`. -/
126	lemma RationalMap.precomp_compHom (f : X ⤏ Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base)
127	    (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
128	    (f.precomp p hp).compHom g = (f.compHom g).precomp p hp := by
129	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
130	  simp only [← RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap, ← RationalMap.compHom_toRationalMap]
131	  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
132	  rw [Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp,
133	    PartialMap.compHom_hom, PartialMap.precomp_hom, PartialMap.precomp_hom,
134	    PartialMap.compHom_hom, Category.assoc]
135	  rfl
136	
137	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
138	/-- Precomposing the rational map of a *morphism* `g : X ⟶ Y` with an open morphism
139	`p : W ⟶ X` is the rational map of the composite `p ≫ g`. This produces the over-`S`
140	hypotheses feeding the pairing of Milne's difference map:
141	`(X.hom.toRationalMap).precomp prᵢ = (prᵢ ≫ X.hom).toRationalMap`. -/
142	@[simp]
143	lemma RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap (g : X ⟶ Y) (p : W ⟶ X)
144	    (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
145	    (g.toRationalMap).precomp p hp = (p ≫ g).toRationalMap := by
146	  rw [show (g.toRationalMap : X ⤏ Y) = g.toPartialMap.toRationalMap from rfl,
147	    ← RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap]
148	  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
149	  simp only [Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp, PartialMap.precomp_hom,
150	    Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap_hom]
151	  have key : (p ∣_ (g.toPartialMap).domain) ≫ X.topIso.hom = W.topIso.hom ≫ p :=
152	    morphismRestrict_ι p _
153	  rw [← Category.assoc, key, Category.assoc]
154	
155	end Scheme
156	
157	end AlgebraicGeometry
158

input.script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'milne33-substep1-api-map',
  description: 'Map the four mathlib API areas needed to assemble Milne 3.3 difference map Φ',
  phases: [{ title: 'Investigate', detail: '4 parallel mathlib-API investigations' }],
}

const MATHLIB = '/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry/.shared-packages/mathlib'
const PROJ = '/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge'

const SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  properties: {
    area: { type: 'string' },
    keyDeclarations: {
      type: 'array',
      items: {
        type: 'object',
        additionalProperties: false,
        properties: {
          name: { type: 'string', description: 'fully qualified Lean name' },
          signature: { type: 'string', description: 'the exact statement/type as written in source' },
          file: { type: 'string' },
          line: { type: 'integer' },
          isInstance: { type: 'boolean', description: 'true if it is an instance (auto-synthesizable)' },
          note: { type: 'string' },
        },
        required: ['name', 'signature', 'file', 'note'],
      },
    },
    recommendedSnippet: { type: 'string', description: 'concrete Lean code snippet the caller can paste to achieve the goal, using the found declarations' },
    caveats: { type: 'string', description: 'gotchas, defeq walls, universe issues, missing lemmas that must be proven by hand' },
    confidence: { type: 'string', enum: ['high', 'medium', 'low'] },
  },
  required: ['area', 'keyDeclarations', 'recommendedSnippet', 'caveats', 'confidence'],
}

const common = `You are investigating mathlib v4.31 source to map a precise API area. Mathlib is at ${MATHLIB} (grep/read its Mathlib/ subtree — deterministic). The consuming project is at ${PROJ}. You may also use the lean LSP MCP tools via ToolSearch (query "select:mcp__lean-lsp__lean_loogle,mcp__lean-lsp__lean_hover_info,mcp__lean-lsp__lean_local_search") but prefer grep/Read of mathlib source for ground truth. Return EXACT declaration names, their verbatim signatures copied from source, file paths, and line numbers. Do not guess names — verify each by reading the source line. Your final output is consumed programmatically as data, not shown to a human.`

phase('Investigate')

const results = await parallel([
  () => agent(`${common}

AREA: "Smooth ⟹ GeometricallyReduced (and → GeometricallyIntegral) for a scheme morphism over a field".

CONTEXT: In ${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean, the theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme has, for X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) with kbar algebraically closed field:
  [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom] [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom] [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
GOAL: I need to conclude IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom) (the product X ×_kbar X). The memory says the ONE genuine new small lemma is "Smooth f ⟹ GeometricallyReduced f" (mathlib v4.31 supposedly has only the converse smooth_of_... / of_grpObj). 

INVESTIGATE and report:
1. The exact name + signature of the morphism-property 'GeometricallyReduced' (and 'GeometricallyIntegral', 'GeometricallyIrreducible', 'GeometricallyConnected') for scheme morphisms. Look under ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/ (Reduced.lean, Integral.lean, Irreducible.lean, Connected.lean).
2. Does mathlib already have "Smooth ⟹ GeometricallyReduced" in ANY form (as instance, lemma, or via 'smooth implies geometrically regular / reduced fibers')? grep for 'GeometricallyReduced', 'Smooth', 'smooth' across Geometrically/ and Morphisms/Smooth*.lean. Check RingHom.Smooth / Algebra.Smooth ⟹ geometrically reduced/regular results too (Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/...). Report the closest existing lemma and whether it needs an algebraically-closed or perfect base.
3. How GeometricallyIntegral is characterized: is there 'geometricallyIntegral_iff' = GeometricallyReduced ∧ GeometricallyIrreducible, or similar? And a lemma IsIntegral X.left ∧ GeometricallyIntegral X.hom over a field ⟹ ... 
4. Report the exact recommendedSnippet for concluding GeometricallyReduced X.hom from [Smooth X.hom] over an algebraically closed field (or note precisely what sub-lemma must be hand-proved and its cleanest route).`, { label: 'geomReduced', phase: 'Investigate', schema: SCHEMA }),

  () => agent(`${common}

AREA: "IsIntegral (pullback f f) — product of an integral scheme with itself over an algebraically closed field".

CONTEXT: same setup as above. X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)), kbar : Field, IsAlgClosed kbar, with [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom] [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom] [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]. I need an instance/term IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom).

The memory pointer is "Geometrically/Integral.lean:108". 

INVESTIGATE and report:
1. Read ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/Integral.lean fully (esp. around line 108). Report every lemma that concludes IsIntegral or GeometricallyIntegral of a pullback / fibre product, with exact signature + hypotheses. Especially: is there something like 'IsIntegral (pullback f g)' from GeometricallyIntegral f + IsIntegral (codomain) + ...? Or 'GeometricallyIntegral.pullback'?
2. What is the exact chain from my hypotheses to IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)? The two factors are both X over Spec kbar. Likely: GeometricallyIntegral X.hom (needs GeometricallyReduced + GeometricallyIrreducible + IsIntegral) ⟹ pullback with the base being an algebraically closed field is integral. Look for the precise mathlib lemma names.
3. Also check Geometrically/Irreducible.lean and Geometrically/Reduced.lean for pullback-stability lemmas (GeometricallyIrreducible is stable under base change; product of geometrically irreducible is irreducible).
4. Report the exact recommendedSnippet (a `haveI`/`have` chain) that yields `IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)` assuming GeometricallyReduced X.hom is available (the other area handles that). Note every instance that must be supplied.`, { label: 'productIntegral', phase: 'Investigate', schema: SCHEMA }),

  () => agent(`${common}

AREA: "Difference morphism d = (g,h) ↦ g·h⁻¹ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G for a group object, in the cartesian-monoidal category Over (Spec kbar), and extracting its underlying scheme morphism".

CONTEXT: G : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) with [GrpObj G] (mathlib's CategoryTheory.GrpObj, ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Grp_.lean:43). The category C = Over (Spec (.of kbar)) is cartesian monoidal. I need the "division" morphism d : G ⊗ G ⟶ G representing (g,h) ↦ g · h⁻¹ (Milne's difference map uses f(x)·f(y)⁻¹). Then I need d.left : (G ⊗ G).left ⟶ G.left, and to know that (G ⊗ G).left = pullback G.hom G.hom (via CategoryTheory.Over.tensorObj_left), and d.w (the over-Spec triangle).

INVESTIGATE and report:
1. In ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Cartesian/Grp_.lean : find the group structure on hom-sets 'Hom.group' (the memory cites 'Monoidal/Cartesian/Grp.lean:59,66'). What is the exact instance name giving Group (X ⟶ G) or a Div/HDiv? Report its signature. Confirm there is a Div instance so that for fst, snd : G ⊗ G ⟶ G one can write `fst / snd : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` and that this equals the pointwise (g,h) ↦ fst·snd⁻¹.
2. In cartesian monoidal categories, what are the exact names of the two projections G ⊗ G ⟶ G? (CartesianMonoidalCategory.fst / .snd — report exact names + signatures from ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Cartesian/Basic.lean). 
3. Alternatively check GrpObj.conj / GrpObj.commutator / any GrpObj.div in Grp_.lean — is there a ready-made 'div : G ⊗ G ⟶ G' = mul ∘ (fst, snd≫inv)? If so report it; else report how to build it: `CartesianMonoidalCategory.lift (fst G G) (snd G G ≫ ι) ≫ MonObj.mul` or `(fst G G) / (snd G G)` using Hom.group.
4. For the Over category: ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Cartesian/Over.lean — confirm Over.tensorObj_left : (X ⊗ Y).left = pullback X.hom Y.hom (or similar), exact name+signature. And how (d : X ⟶ Y in Over S).left and .w work (CategoryTheory.Comma/Over.Hom fields). 
5. Report recommendedSnippet: given [GrpObj G] on G : Over (Spec kbar), produce `d : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` and its `.left : pullback G.hom G.hom ⟶ G.left` and note how to discharge the over-Spec condition from `d.w`.`, { label: 'diffMorphism', phase: 'Investigate', schema: SCHEMA }),

  () => agent(`${common}

AREA: "Openness of the first projection: IsOpenMap (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).base for X.hom smooth over a field".

CONTEXT: X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)), [Smooth X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]. I need `IsOpenMap (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).base` (the topological openness of the first projection of the self-product) to feed RationalMap.precomp. The memory chain is: Smooth X.hom ⟹ Smooth (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom) [Smooth.lean:187] ⟹ Flat + LocallyOfFinitePresentation ⟹ UniversallyOpen [UniversallyOpen.lean:145] ⟹ IsOpenMap.

INVESTIGATE and report:
1. In ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Smooth.lean (around line 187): the instance/lemma that Smooth is stable under base change / pullback. Exact name + signature. Does `Smooth (pullback.fst f g)` synthesize as an instance from `[Smooth g]` (MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange / pullback_fst)? Report exact names.
2. The chain Smooth ⟹ Flat and Smooth ⟹ LocallyOfFinitePresentation (or LocallyOfFiniteType): exact instance names in Smooth.lean / RingHom. 
3. In ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/UniversallyOpen.lean (around line 145): the lemma giving UniversallyOpen from Flat + LocallyOfFinitePresentation (this is 'flat + locally of finite presentation ⟹ open', EGA). Exact name + signature + exact hypotheses.
4. How to go from UniversallyOpen f (or just the morphism property) to `IsOpenMap f.base`: the lemma UniversallyOpen ⟹ IsOpenMap, exact name (e.g. UniversallyOpen.isOpenMap or @[instance]). 
5. Report recommendedSnippet: the exact `have hopen : IsOpenMap (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).base := ...` term/tactic block, listing which steps are instance-synthesis (haveI/inferInstance) vs explicit lemma application. Note if any hypothesis (e.g. LocallyOfFinitePresentation vs LocallyOfFiniteType, or QuasiCompact) is missing from my context and must be added.`, { label: 'openness', phase: 'Investigate', schema: SCHEMA }),
])

return results

246	private theorem av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
247	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
248	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
249	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
250	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
251	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
252	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
253	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
254	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
255	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left)
256	    (hover : f.compHom A.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
257	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ := by
258	  -- Materialise the variety-package instances on `A.left` needed by Milne
259	  -- Lemma 3.3 and Theorem 3.1: `IsIntegral` from the split helper
260	  -- `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
261	  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
262	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
263	  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
264	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
265	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
266	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
267	  -- Milne Lemma 3.3: the locus is empty, or every point of it specialises
268	  -- from a coheight-1 point of the locus.
269	  rcases indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f hover with hempty | hpure
270	  · exact hempty
271	  · -- Milne 3.1 (codim-≥2) contradicts any coheight-1 point of the locus.
272	    rw [Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_notMem]
273	    intro x hx
274	    obtain ⟨z, hzZ, hz1, -⟩ := hpure x hx
275	    have h2 := indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete f hover z hzZ
276	    rw [hz1] at h2
277	    norm_num at h2
278	
279	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
280	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
281	
282	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
283	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
284	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
285	
286	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
287	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
288	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
289	`J → A`.
290	
291	**Body (run-0006 T6, session 0015 refactor).** The proof is Milne's
292	two-line argument, run through the honest `Z(f) = ∅` intermediate:
293	
294	1. `av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (above) combines Milne Lemma 3.3
295	   (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` — the single remaining
296	   `sorry` of this chain) with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1
297	   (`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete`, proved axiom-clean) to
298	   force `indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
299	
300	2. `existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (proved axiom-clean in
301	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): a rational map from a reduced scheme
302	   to a separated scheme with empty indeterminacy locus is uniquely
303	   represented by a regular morphism. The scheme-level separatedness
304	   `A.left.IsSeparated` is derived from `IsSeparated A.hom` (from
305	   `IsProper A.hom`) composed with the affine `Spec k̄ ⟶ ⊤_ Scheme`.
306	
307	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
308	content is concentrated in Milne Lemma 3.3. -/
309	theorem extend_to_av
310	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
311	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
312	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
313	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
314	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
315	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
316	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
317	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
318	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left)
319	    (hover : f.compHom A.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
320	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
321	  -- Scheme-level separatedness of the target (`A.hom` is separated since
322	  -- proper, and `Spec k̄` is affine hence separated over the terminal).
323	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
324	  haveI : A.left.IsSeparated := by
325	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
326	    have heq : terminal.from A.left = A.hom ≫ terminal.from _ :=
327	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
328	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
329	  -- Milne's argument: the indeterminacy locus is empty, then extend.
330	  exact existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty f
331	    (av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty f hover)
332	
333	end RationalMap
334	
335	end Scheme

337	end AlgebraicGeometry

130	-- caveats --
131	1) INSTANCE SCOPE: `Hom.group` and `Hom.monoid` are instances ONLY under `open scoped CategoryTheory.MonObj` (Cartesian/Grp_.lean:66, Cartesian/Mon_.lean:209). Without it, `fst G G / snd G G`, `*`, `⁻¹` on hom-sets will NOT elaborate. 2) NO built-in division morphism: `GrpObj.conj` (x·y·x⁻¹) and `GrpObj.commutator` (x·y·x⁻¹·y⁻¹) are the only prebuilt G⊗G⟶G maps; neither is x·y⁻¹. Use `fst G G / snd G G`. 3) `f / g = f * g⁻¹` is NOT set as a custom field of Hom.group — it comes from the DivInvMonoid default; rewrite with `div_eq_mul_inv` (it is propositional, generally not rfl). Then `Hom.mul_def`+`Hom.inv_def` give `lift (fst) (snd ≫ ι) ≫ μ`. 4) The CMC on `Over (Spec kbar)` is GLOBAL for Schemes (AlgebraicGeometry/Pullbacks.lean:689), so no local-instance ceremony — but you must import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullbacks. In generic mathlib (Cartesian/Over.lean) it is only a local instance. 5) `.left` does NOT auto-distribute over `/`, `*`, `⁻¹` (the forget functor isn't registered as division-preserving in the elaborator); unfold via `div_eq_mul_inv`/`Hom.mul_def`/`Hom.inv_def` first, then `simp [Over.comp_left, Over.lift_left, Over.fst_left, Over.snd_left]`. 6) `(G ⊗ G).left = pullback G.hom G.hom` and `(fst/snd G G).left = pullback.fst/snd` are all `rfl`/@[simp], so definitional transport is cheap. 7) `Over.w` direction: for `φ : f ⟶ g`, it is `φ.left ≫ g.hom = f.hom`; hence `d.w : d.left ≫ G.hom = (G ⊗ G).hom` (not the reverse). 8) `ι.left` in the explicit d.left form is `ι[G].left = GrpObj.inv.left : pullback G.hom G.hom → ... ` wait: ι : G ⟶ G so ι.left : G.left ⟶ G.left; the pullback.condition side-goal uses `Hom.inv_def`/`Over.w ι`. Adjust the `by simp` side-condition if the exact defeq for the lift's compatibility proof differs in your context.

The final instance is Integral.lean:104 `[GeometricallyIntegral f][Flat f][UniversallyOpen f][IsIntegral Y][IsLocallyNoetherian Y] : IsIntegral (pullback f g)` (symmetric twin at :108). \n\nEXACTLY TWO haveI are required; everything else is auto: (1) `GeometricallyIntegral X.hom` via the LEMMA (not instance) GeometricallyIntegral.of_geometricallyReduced_of_geometricallyIrreducible (Integral.lean:61) — needs GeometricallyReduced X.hom + GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom; (2) `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left` via the THEOREM (not instance) LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom (Noetherian.lean:237). The task states to assume it available. \n\nNoetherian-synthesis subtlety: IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec(.of kbar)) needs IsNoetherianRing kbar. For a Field this resolves through instance chain Field.toEuclideanDomain (priority-100 instance, EuclideanDomain/Field.lean:24) → EuclideanDomain.to_principal_ideal_domain (PrincipalIdealDomain.lean:266) → PrincipalIdealRing.isNoetherianRing (PrincipalIdealDomain.lean:111); a parallel path is DivisionRing.instIsArtinianRing (Artinian/Module.lean:376). Should synthesize, but if it ever stalls, add `haveI : IsNoetherianRing (CommRingCat.of kbar) := inferInstanceAs (IsNoetherianRing kbar)` before the base-change step. \n\nNamespace: all names live in `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`. `open CategoryTheory Limits` needed for `pullback`. So a genuine hand-proof of the morphism property would be substantial. (2) BUT this is moot: the consuming project ALREADY closed the reducedness content as the PUBLIC axiom-clean lemma `isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` (CodimOneExtension.lean:881, in the SAME file, before the target theorem at 1662), backed by `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` / SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean (Stacks 00TT). The recommended route uses it directly on the self-product and never constructs `GeometricallyReduced X.hom` at all.\n\nDEFEQ/PACKAGING GOTCHAS: (a) `isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` needs `[Smooth (Over.mk g).hom]`; `(Over.mk g).hom` is `rfl`-equal to `g`, so instance search should pick up `hsm`, but if it stalls, pass it explicitly (`@isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed kbar _ _ (Over.mk _) hsm`). (b) `pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom` and `pullback.snd X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom` are equal only up to `pullback.condition`; pick fst (its smoothness is the direct instance) and stick with it — do not mix. (c) The `IrreducibleSpace ↥(pullback f g)` instance (Irreducible.lean:108) requires `IrreducibleSpace Y` where Y is the SOURCE of the SECOND arg g; with f=g=X.hom this is X.left, supplied by `irreducibleSpace_of_isIntegral` from `IsIntegral X.left`. `UniversallyOpen X.hom` is available two ways (Subsingleton+IsIntegral base, or Flat+lfp), both low-priority instances — if inference is sluggish add `haveI : UniversallyOpen X.hom := inferInstance` before step 3.\n\nDo NOT try the `IsReduced (pullback f g)` instance in Reduced.lean:121 — it additionally requires `[Flat f] [IsReduced Y] [IsLocallyNoetherian Y]` AND `[GeometricallyReduced f]`, i.e. it needs the very morphism property you are avoiding; the `isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` route is cleaner.

input.content:
/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.RationalMapPrecomp
import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.RationalMapProd

/-!
# Milne's difference rational map `Φ = (x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`

This file assembles **Sub-step 1** of Milne's Lemma 3.3
(`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
from a rational map `f : X ⤏ G` into a group variety over an algebraically closed
field `k̄`, build the *difference rational map*

  `Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G`,   `Φ(x, y) = f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`,

which is the vehicle for reducing the indeterminacy of `f` to a pole-divisor
computation on the nonsingular surface `X ×_{k̄} X`.

The construction glues the two rational-map bricks already in the project:

* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp` (`Albanese/RationalMapPrecomp.lean`) — precompose
  `f` with the two projections `prᵢ : X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X`, which are open because
  `X.hom` is smooth (hence its base change `prᵢ` is smooth, flat, locally of finite
  presentation, universally open, an open map);
* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod` (`Albanese/RationalMapProd.lean`) — pair
  `f ∘ pr₁` and `f ∘ pr₂` into `X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G ×_{k̄} G`;

and then composes on the right with the **group-object difference morphism**
`GrpObj.diff G = (g, h) ↦ g · h⁻¹ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G`, whose underlying scheme morphism
`(GrpObj.diff G).left : G ×_{k̄} G ⟶ G` is the honest group law
`m ∘ (id × inv)`.

## Main definitions

* `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.diff` — the division morphism `G ⊗ G ⟶ G` of a group
  object in any cartesian-monoidal category.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.differenceRationalMap` — Milne's
  `Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G`.

## Main results

* `AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_pullback_self` — the self-product `X ×_{k̄} X` of a
  smooth geometrically-irreducible integral `X/k̄` (`k̄` algebraically closed) is
  integral. (Also the input the pole-purity engine of Sub-step 4 needs.)
* `AlgebraicGeometry.isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self` / `..._snd_self` — the two
  projections of the self-product are open maps.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.differenceRationalMap_compHom_over` — the
  difference map is a `k̄`-rational map.

Sub-steps 2 (`(x, x) ∈ Dom Φ ↔ x ∈ Dom f`) and 4b (diagonal codim-1 Krull bound)
remain; they consume `differenceRationalMap` built here.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonoidalCategory Limits TopologicalSpace
open scoped CategoryTheory.MonObj

namespace CategoryTheory.GrpObj

variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]

/-- **Difference / division morphism of a group object.** For a group object `G`
in a cartesian-monoidal category, `GrpObj.diff G = fst / snd : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` is the
morphism `(g, h) ↦ g · h⁻¹`, i.e. the group law composed with inversion on the
second factor. There is no ready-made such morphism in mathlib (`GrpObj.conj` and
`GrpObj.commutator` are the only prebuilt `G ⊗ G ⟶ G` maps), so we build it from
the hom-set group structure `CategoryTheory.MonObj.Hom.group`. -/
noncomputable def diff (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ⟶ G :=
  fst G G / snd G G

/-- `GrpObj.diff G` unfolds to the explicit `lift (fst) (snd ≫ ι) ≫ μ`. -/
lemma diff_eq (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
    diff G = lift (fst G G) (snd G G ≫ ι) ≫ μ := by
  rw [diff, div_eq_mul_inv, Hom.mul_def, Hom.inv_def]

end CategoryTheory.GrpObj

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]

/-- The underlying scheme morphism of the group-object difference morphism sits
over `Spec k̄`: `(GrpObj.diff G).left ≫ G.hom = pr₁ ≫ G.hom`. This is Milne's
`Φ.compHom G.hom` reduction, using `Over.w`. -/
lemma grpObjDiff_left_comp_hom (G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [GrpObj G] :
    (GrpObj.diff G).left ≫ G.hom = pullback.fst G.hom G.hom ≫ G.hom := by
  simpa using Over.w (GrpObj.diff G)

variable [IsAlgClosed kbar]

/-- **Integrality of the self-product.** For a smooth, geometrically irreducible,
integral scheme `X` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, the self fibre product
`X ×_{k̄} X` is integral: it is reduced because it is smooth over the algebraically
closed field (`isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` on the composite structure map)
and irreducible because `X.hom` is geometrically irreducible and universally open
(`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace`). -/
theorem isIntegral_pullback_self (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom] [IsIntegral X.left] :
    IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom) := by
  haveI hsm : Smooth (Over.mk (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom)).hom := inferInstance
  haveI hred : IsReduced (pullback X.hom X.hom) :=
    isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed (Over.mk (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom))
  haveI hirr : IrreducibleSpace (pullback X.hom X.hom) := inferInstance
  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _

/-- The first projection `X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X` is an open map (it is smooth, being a
base change of the smooth structure morphism, hence universally open). -/
theorem isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [Smooth X.hom] :
    IsOpenMap (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).base :=
  (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).isOpenMap

/-- The second projection `X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X` is an open map. -/
theorem isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [Smooth X.hom] :
    IsOpenMap (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom).base :=
  (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom).isOpenMap

namespace Scheme.RationalMap

variable {X G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
  [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
  [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
  [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
  [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
  [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]

/-- **Milne's difference rational map** `Φ = (x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`.

For a rational map `f : X ⤏ G` into a group variety over `k̄`, with `f` defined
over `k̄` (`f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap`), the difference map
`Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G` is `((f ∘ pr₁) × (f ∘ pr₂)) ∘ (g, h ↦ g · h⁻¹)`. -/
noncomputable def differenceRationalMap
    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
    (pullback X.hom X.hom).RationalMap G.left :=
  haveI := isIntegral_pullback_self X
  (RationalMap.prod (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom) G.hom G.hom
      (f.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X))
      (f.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X))
      (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap])
      (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap,
        pullback.condition])).compHom (GrpObj.diff G).left

/-- **The difference map is a `k̄`-rational map.** Right-composing `Φ` with the
structure morphism `G.hom` recovers the structure morphism `pr₁ ≫ X.hom` of
`X ×_{k̄} X`. -/
theorem differenceRationalMap_compHom_over
    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
    (differenceRationalMap f hover).compHom G.hom
      = (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom).toRationalMap := by
  haveI := isIntegral_pullback_self X
  rw [differenceRationalMap, RationalMap.compHom_compHom, grpObjDiff_left_comp_hom]
  exact RationalMap.prod_compHom_over _ _ _ _ _ _ _

end Scheme.RationalMap

end AlgebraicGeometry
